summary: date-fns-lite postinstall exfiltrates cloud IAM credentials and Kubernetes service account tokens
details: Package presents itself as a lightweight date-formatting utility (typosquatting the popular date-fns library) but its postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and performs cloud credential theft. The script collects host identity (hostname, user, network interfaces, selected env vars, process info), probes cloud instance-metadata services at 169.254.169.254 (AWS, including the IMDSv2 token dance to reach /iam/security-credentials/), 100.100.100.200 (Aliyun RAM, Tencent CAM, Huawei), and metadata.google.internal (GCP default service-account token), and reads the in-pod Kubernetes service account token at /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token. The collected report is base64-encoded and split into 200/400-byte chunks embedded as URL path segments (`/idx-total-part`), then sent via both node http.get and curl to a hardcoded attacker host at http://792a54c8.fenqi.eyes.sh. Any CI or cloud environment that installs this package leaks role credentials and cluster tokens to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
